summary: The project will be updated to bundle all project data (JSON, PDF, and images) into a ZIP file upon export. The import process will be enhanced to support both ZIP files and directory uploads, allowing assets to be resolved from relative paths as requested.

plan.title: Add ZIP library
plan.description: Add `jszip` and its type definitions to `package.json` to enable bundle creation and extraction in the browser.

plan.title: Update Export Logic
plan.description: Modify `useExport.ts` to generate a ZIP file. The ZIP will contain `project.json`, the source PDF file, and an `assets/` folder for images and custom icons. Update the JSON structure to use relative paths for these assets instead of embedding large Base64 strings.

plan.title: Update Import Interface
plan.description: Enhance `PDFUploader.tsx` to handle `.zip` files and directory selections (`webkitdirectory`). Implement logic to resolve asset paths from the uploaded bundle or directory and convert them back to usable URLs (Data/Blob) for the editor state.

plan.title: Maintain Backward Compatibility
plan.description: Ensure the `IMPORT_PROJECT` reducer action and the import logic in `PDFUploader.tsx` still support legacy standalone `.json` files containing embedded Base64 assets.

acceptance_criteria.description: Exporting a project generates a `.zip` file containing `project.json`, `document.pdf`, and an `assets/` directory.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Trigger export in the UI and inspect the downloaded ZIP file structure.

acceptance_criteria.description: `project.json` inside the ZIP uses relative paths (e.g., 'assets/image_1.png') for image content instead of Base64.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Extract the ZIP and check the `content` fields in the `project.json` file.

acceptance_criteria.description: Importing the generated ZIP file restores the full project state including the PDF and all visual objects.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Upload a previously exported ZIP via the 'Import' tool and verify layers, icons, and images appear correctly.

acceptance_criteria.description: Importing a folder (directory upload) containing the extracted ZIP content correctly resolves assets.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Use the directory upload option, select an unzipped project folder, and verify all assets load correctly.

acceptance_criteria.description: Legacy `.json` files with embedded Base64 data can still be imported.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Import an old `project.json` file and verify it loads without errors.

acceptance_criteria.description: The PDF file is automatically loaded into the viewer when a ZIP project is imported.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Import a ZIP and ensure the canvas displays the correct PDF document immediately.

risks: High memory consumption when Zipping very large PDF files or many high-resolution images.
Dependency on `jszip` which must be compatible with the current build environment.
Directory upload (`webkitdirectory`) is not supported in all browsers (e.g., older versions or specific mobile browsers).
